offline.message: You are offline. Emot-ID is using cached content on this device.

analyze.buttonDisabledSomatic: Tap a body area where you notice a sensation
analyze.buttonDisabledDimensional: Tap the square where your state fits on the two axes
analyze.buttonDisabledDefault: Select an emotion that resonates with you
analyze.aiPrompt: I feel {emotions}. What does this emotion mean and how can I understand it better?
analyze.aiPromptMultiple: I feel a combination of {emotions}. How do these emotions relate and what might this mean?
analyze.aiWarning: This will search for general information online. Results are not a substitute for professional support.
analyze.externalAIDisabled: External AI search is off. You can enable it in Settings.

crisis.tier1: If what you're feeling feels overwhelming, support is available. You don't have to navigate this alone.
crisis.tier2: It sounds like you may be going through a difficult time. You don't have to go through it alone.
crisis.tier3: What you're describing sounds very painful. You deserve support right now — please consider reaching out.
crisis.tier4: If you are thinking about ending your life, please reach out now. You deserve help.
crisis.temporalNote: We noticed this pattern appearing more often lately. That's okay — it's information, not a judgment.
crisis.disclaimer: If you are in immediate danger, please call emergency services.
crisis.groundingBody: Name 5 things you see, 4 you can touch, 3 you hear, 2 you smell, 1 you taste. This helps bring your attention to the present moment.

onboarding.screen1Title: This is an exploration, not a test
onboarding.screen1Body: There are no right or wrong answers. Emotions are complex and layered. Take your time — this is a space for curiosity, not judgment.
onboarding.screen1BodySimple: There is no right or wrong answer. Be curious, not harsh with yourself.
onboarding.screen2Body: No emotion is good or bad. Anger protects boundaries. Sadness helps process loss. Fear keeps you safe. Instead of asking "what's wrong," ask "what is my body telling me?"
onboarding.screen2BodySimple: Each emotion has a job. None are bad. Ask: what is this feeling trying to tell me?
onboarding.screen3Body: Body Map — feel where emotions live in your body. Emotion Wheel — navigate from general to specific feelings. Plutchik — discover how emotions combine and interact. Each reveals different aspects. Start wherever feels natural.
onboarding.screen3BodySimple: Body Map: start with sensations. Emotion Wheel: choose feeling words. Plutchik: see emotion combinations. Pick what feels easiest.
onboarding.screen4Body: This app supports emotional self-awareness. It is not a diagnostic tool and does not replace professional mental health support. If you ever want to talk to someone, you can call 116 123 (Romania, free, 24/7) or visit findahelpline.com.
onboarding.screen4BodySimple: This app helps you notice emotions. It is not medical diagnosis. If you need support, call 116 123 or visit findahelpline.com.

reminders.label: Daily reminder
reminders.unsupported: Notifications are not supported on this device.
reminders.permissionDenied: Notifications are blocked. Enable them in browser settings.
reminders.notificationTitle: Time for a quick emotional check-in
reminders.notificationBody: Take 30 seconds to notice what you feel.

firstHint.somatic: Tap where you notice a sensation
firstHint.wheel: Tap an emotion that resonates with you
firstHint.plutchik: Tap an emotion to see how it connects
firstHint.dimensional: Tap where your state feels located

somatic.guidedStart: Take a breath. Notice your body.
somatic.guidedPrompt: What do you notice in your {region}?
somatic.guidedTraumaNote: If any area feels uncomfortable, you can skip it at any time.
somatic.guidedNothingNormal: Not noticing sensations is common. Body awareness develops with practice.
somatic.interoceptionTip: Body awareness is like a muscle — it develops with practice. Try placing your hand on your stomach and just noticing the temperature.
somatic.interoceptionTip2: You can practice noticing sensations during everyday activities: the warmth of a cup, the weight of your feet on the floor.
somatic.interoceptionTip3: If nothing comes up, that's okay. Sometimes the signal is 'neutral' — and noticing neutral is also body awareness.
somatic.guidedPause: You noticed strong {sensation} in your {region}. Take a breath before continuing.
somatic.numbnessFlooding: Your body may be trying to protect you right now. Would you like to try a grounding exercise before continuing?

granularity.title: Notice the difference
granularity.lede: Compare similar feeling words. There is no score.
granularity.notSure: I'm not sure — they all fit
granularity.feedbackSelected: You chose {emotion}. If that feels closest to your experience, your choice is valid.
granularity.feedbackNotSure: It's okay not to be sure. Emotional nuance develops with practice.

distinctions.intensity: These options can differ mostly by intensity, from mild to strong.
distinctions.duration: These options can differ by duration and by how deeply the feeling settles in.
distinctions.focus: These options can differ by focus: inner values, social image, or a wish to repair.
distinctions.time: These options can differ by time orientation: present interest, active exploration, or future readiness.

granularity.completedBody: You just practiced noticing subtle differences between similar emotions.
granularity.completedEncouragement: This skill grows through repetition, not perfection.
granularity.errorBody: Practice sets are unavailable right now. Please try again later.

chainAnalysis.title: Unpack a moment
chainAnalysis.prompt: Walk through one situation, one step at a time. Short answers are enough.
chainAnalysis.triggeringEvent: What happened right before this started?
chainAnalysis.triggeringEventPlaceholder: Example: I got a message from my manager.
chainAnalysis.vulnerabilityFactors: What made you more vulnerable today?
chainAnalysis.vulnerabilityFactorsPlaceholder: Example: low sleep, skipped meals, conflict, stress.
chainAnalysis.promptingEvent: What was the exact prompting event?
chainAnalysis.promptingEventPlaceholder: Describe the specific moment, not the whole day.
chainAnalysis.emotion: What emotion did you feel most strongly?
chainAnalysis.saved: This reflection is now in your journal.

dimensional.instructions: Locate your state using energy and pleasantness

navigation.offline: Offline. Everything here still works on this device.

today.title: How are you feeling?
today.lede: Choose the closest feeling below, or explore it in more detail.
today.quickPrompt: One of these may be close enough for now.
today.recentEmpty: Your reflections can stay here when you choose to save them.

arrival.title: What feels easiest to notice?
arrival.lede: You can change routes at any time. A rough starting point is enough.
arrival.wordsHint: Move from a broad feeling toward something more precise.
arrival.bodyHint: Notice a region, sensation, and intensity before naming it.
arrival.affectHint: Use energy and pleasantness when words are not available.
arrival.unsureHint: Begin with one simple choice and see what becomes clearer.
arrival.guideBodyTitle: Can you point to where you feel something in your body?
arrival.guideBodyHint: A general area is enough. You do not need to know what it means yet.
arrival.guideBodyYes: Yes, I can point to an area
arrival.guideBodyYesHint: Start with the location and describe the sensation.
arrival.guideBodyNotClearHint: Try placing the feeling without naming it.
arrival.guidePlacementTitle: Can you roughly place the feeling?
arrival.guidePlacementHint: Think about energy and pleasantness. No emotion word is needed.
arrival.guidePlacementYesHint: Use the map to choose an approximate position.
arrival.guideWordsHint: Start with a general feeling and narrow it down.

bodyCompass.title: Where do you notice it?
bodyCompass.lede: A sensation can offer clues, but it does not prove any one emotion.
bodyCompass.regionHint: Tap one area. You can add more after reviewing it.
bodyCompass.sensationHint: Choose the closest description for your {region}.
bodyCompass.intensityHint: Use the closest estimate. Precision is not required.
bodyCompass.reviewLede: Change anything that does not feel right before seeing possible words.
bodyCompass.notNow: I do not notice anything right now

affectMap.title: Place the feeling
affectMap.lede: Tap where your current energy and pleasantness seem closest. Approximate is useful.

wordLadder.title: Move toward a closer word
wordLadder.lede: Start broad. You may stop at any level when a word feels close enough.
wordLadder.compareHint: Choose one word from the same level. The descriptions may help; neither word has to fit.
wordLadder.compareNote: Notice which description, if either, feels closer.
wordLadder.noDescription: No description is available for this word yet.

plutchik.title: Combine two feelings
plutchik.lede: Choose two primary emotions. The relationship is a possibility, not a diagnosis.
plutchik.instructions: Choose the two emotions that feel closest.
plutchik.fieldLabel: Eight primary emotions arranged as a wheel

reflectionScreen.title: What may be here
reflectionScreen.needPrompt: What feels most needed right now?
reflectionScreen.needHint: Keep the suggestion that fits best, or leave this open.
reflectionScreen.closeBody: You noticed something real. That is enough for now.

exploreScreen.title: Different ways to understand a feeling
exploreScreen.lede: Each method notices something different. None is the master view.
exploreScreen.bodyHint: Start with where a sensation appears.
exploreScreen.affectHint: Place a state before trying to name it.
exploreScreen.wordsHint: Move from broad language to a closer word.
exploreScreen.plutchikHint: Choose two emotions and see a possible blend.
exploreScreen.practiceHint: Compare nearby words through short examples.

journalScreen.title: Your emotional thread
journalScreen.lede: Patterns are observations, not scores. Everything remains on this device.
journalScreen.emptyBody: When saving is on, completed check-ins will appear here.
journalScreen.errorTitle: Saved reflections could not be loaded
journalScreen.errorBody: Your local data was not changed. Close and reopen the app to try again.

sessionDetail.title: Saved reflection
sessionDetail.older: This reflection was saved before these details were available.

privacyData.title: Privacy & data
privacyData.lede: Your reflections are stored locally. Emot-ID has no account, analytics, or cloud sync.
privacyData.externalHint: Opening one of these links sends selected words to Google Search.
privacyData.confirmClear: This removes saved reflections, journal exercises, and preferences from this device. It cannot be undone.
privacyData.clearSuccess: Local data was deleted and preferences were reset.
privacyData.dataError: The action could not be completed. Reopen the app and try again.

supportScreen.title: Support
supportScreen.lede: Emot-ID supports self-reflection. It is not therapy, diagnosis, or emergency care.
supportScreen.urgent: If you may be in immediate danger, call local emergency services now.

history.title: Past Sessions
history.suggestSomatic: Explore your body's signals with the Body Map
history.suggestDimensional: Map your feelings on a 2D space
history.suggestWheel: Name emotions more precisely with the Wheel
history.suggestPlutchik: Discover how emotions connect and combine
history.progressionSomatic: You've been exploring body sensations. The Emotion Wheel can help you put names to what you've noticed.
history.progressionWheel: You've been naming emotions. The Body Map can show you where they live in your body.
history.progressionPlutchik: You've been exploring combinations. The Emotional Space can show you the bigger picture.
history.progressionDimensional: You've been mapping your emotional space. The Body Map connects these feelings to physical sensations.
